key_metrics: Gross Profit: $1.04 billion, down 15.8% YoY from $1.23 billion
Inventories: $1.71 billion, up 36.2% from $1.25 billion at year-end 2024
qoq_changes: Significant inventory buildup, increasing by $453 million since December 2024.
Major debt restructuring, with short-term debt decreasing by $1.1 billion and long-term debt increasing by $1.5 billion since year-end.
new_risks: Severe cost of sales inflation driving a 15.8% drop in gross profit despite top-line revenue growth.
Massive unrealized losses on derivative contracts ($541.1 million in 9M 2025 vs a $200.3 million gain in 9M 2024), likely tied to commodity hedging such as cocoa.
management_tone: Cautious, as solid top-line sales growth is being heavily overshadowed by severe commodity cost inflation and margin deterioration.
analyst_note: Hershey delivered 6.5% top-line growth, but surging cost of sales and massive derivative losses crushed profitability, driving a 38% YoY decline in net income. The $450M inventory build and long-term debt refinancing suggest management is bracing for prolonged commodity market volatility.
